_comment: WC2026 Group C, MD3. Standings before: Brazil 4, Morocco 4 (1-1 Brazil, 1-0 Scotland), Scotland 3, Haiti 0 (0-1 Scotland, 0-3 Brazil; 0 goals, conceded 4). Haiti is GENUINELY WEAK and open (no goals, leaky) -> the favourite+goals archetype (like Saudi / Uzbekistan), NOT a parked low block. Morocco the clear quality side and wins comfortably, BUT Morocco is itself low-scoring/pragmatic (only 2 goals in two games), so expect a controlled win rather than a Spain/Portugal-style blowout. Home = Morocco, away = Haiti. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Morocco (4 pts) qualifies with a win; Haiti (0 pts) is virtually eliminated. Morocco a strong favourite vs a weak, beaten Haiti.

home.name: Morocco
notes: Quality, organized; beat Scotland 1-0, drew Brazil 1-1. Efficient but low-scoring (2 GF in two games). attack 1.30.
Strong defense vs a blunt Haiti. defense 0.85.
Clear favourite; controlled win more likely than a blowout.

away.name: Haiti
notes: Genuinely weak and open: 0 goals, conceded 4 (0-1 Scotland, 0-3 Brazil). attack 0.55.
Virtually eliminated, open side -> favourite + goals, not a low block.

_postmatch.result: Morocco 4-2 Haiti. Morocco won but it was a 6-goal game; Haiti scored twice.
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit -- the 70% favourite won. BUT the GOALS model missed badly: I called a controlled low-scoring win (lean Under 2.5 at 64%, BTTS No 68%, Morocco clean sheet 62%) and it was 4-2 -- six goals, both scoring, no clean sheet. Morocco overperformed (1.82 -> 4) AND Haiti, modelled as near-toothless (lambda 0.48 after 0 goals in two games), scored 2.
_postmatch.lesson: Dead-rubber GOAL VOLUME is a trap: a low-stakes final-round game can blow open into a goal-fest even when both sides looked low-scoring. Don't over-trust a tiny lambda for a 'weak' side (0.48 implied ~0.5 goals; they scored 2) and don't lean Under just because the favourite had been pragmatic. The 1X2 was safe; the goals texture was the opposite of the model. Mirror of England-Ghana (there a favourite was held to 0-0; here a controlled win became 4-2) -- final-round texture is high-variance, trust the result read over the goals read.
